summary: The translation correctly renders the section title per the glossary and successfully converts Dutch Scripture references to standard English forms, including versification adjustments. However, it violates the preservation requirement by adding vowel points to unpointed Hebrew spans and fails to translate Dutch bibliographic abbreviations such as 'Wijs. Godsd.' and 'G. v. I.', leaving them unintelligible to the target audience. The opening sentence also suffers from a calqued translation that obscures the original meaning.

preserved-language-integrity.details: Hebrew spans were modified by the addition of vowel points not present in the source excerpt (e.g., source 'אלהים' vs translation 'אֱלֹהִים'; source 'אלה' vs translation 'אָלַה').

glossary-adherence.details: The section title term 'EIGENNAMEN GODS' is correctly rendered as 'proper names of God' in the translation header.

prose-quality.details: Dutch bibliographic abbreviations (e.g., 'Wijs. Godsd.', 'G. v. I.') were left untranslated, and the opening sentence uses the awkward calque 'distinguish themselves' for 'zonderen zich af', flattening the argument structure.

review-flagging.details: Findings explicitly identify the preservation corruption, the drift in handling Dutch abbreviations, and the prose quality issue.

findings.detail: The translator added vowel points to Hebrew words that appear as unpointed consonants in the source text (e.g., 'אלהים' became 'אֱלֹהִים'; 'אלה' became 'אָלַה'), violating the requirement to preserve spans exactly.

Dutch bibliographic abbreviations 'Wijs. Godsd.' (Wijsgeerige Godsdienst) and 'G. v. I.' (Godsdienst van Israël) were preserved in the English text without translation, contrary to the style guide requirement to translate Dutch abbreviations.

The phrase 'distinguish themselves' in the opening sentence is a semantic calque of 'zonderen zich af'; the correct rendering in this context is 'are set apart' or 'are distinguished', which clarifies the theological argument.

recommended_follow_up: Restore all Hebrew spans to their exact unpointed forms as found in the source excerpt.
Translate the Dutch abbreviations 'Wijs. Godsd.' and 'G. v. I.' into their English equivalents (e.g., 'Philosophy of Religion', 'Religion of Israel').
Revise the first sentence to read '...those names are clearly set apart in the first place...'.
